output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 3/3.
Batch 1: Completed batch 1 for `T1` and `T2`. Added video file detection, added generation-backed video upload with poster extraction and duration propagation, and wrote the batch checkpoint to `.megaplan/plans/register-uploaded-dropped-20260406-2144/execution_batch_1.json`. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed.
Batch 2: Completed batch 2 for `T3`. Video files now route through `uploadVideoGeneration` in the external drop flow, the hook interfaces are wired end-to-end, and the existing `useExternalDrop` test module was updated to assert the new video-generation path. `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx` both passed.
Batch 3: Completed batch 3 for `T4`. Final verification passed: TypeScript compiled cleanly, the full existing `useExternalDrop` test module passed, and a temporary script confirmed that dropped `.mp4` files now take the generation-backed video path and do not fall through to `uploadAsset`. The temporary script was deleted after it passed.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `isVideoFile` next to `isImageFile`, matching `video/` MIME types and the planned `.mp4`, `.mov`, `.webm`, `.m4v`, and `.avi` extensions. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` after the change.

Added `uploadVideoGeneration` using `extractVideoPosterFrame`, `uploadBlobToStorage`, and `extractVideoMetadata`, with thumbnail/metadata fallbacks that do not block upload. Widened generation registration to carry optional `durationSeconds` and persist it onto `AssetRegistryEntry.duration`. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`.

Wired `uploadVideoGeneration` through `useExternalDrop` into `handleFileDrop`, and inserted the video branch immediately after the image branch and before the generic `uploadAsset` fallback. Re-verified that `registerGenerationAsset` already infers video MIME types from `content_type`, `variantType`, or video extensions, so no further change was needed there. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx`.

Verified the final state with `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` and the only existing matching test module, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx`, which passed in full. No existing `useAssetManagement` or `external-drop-utils` test modules were present. A temporary script exercised `handleFileDrop` with a dropped `.mp4` file and confirmed the video path calls `uploadVideoGeneration`, skips `uploadAsset`, and settles pending ops; the script was then deleted.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `isVideoFile` now accepts both `file.type.startsWith('video/')` and the required `.mp4`, `.mov`, `.webm`, `.m4v`, `.avi` extensions.

`uploadVideoGeneration` uses `extractVideoPosterFrame` plus `uploadBlobToStorage` for thumbnails, falls back to the uploaded video URL on thumbnail failure, and extracts `duration_seconds` via `extractVideoMetadata` for registry propagation.

The new `isVideoFile` branch sits directly after the existing `isImageFile` branch and directly before the generic `uploadAsset` path, so image uploads keep their current behavior and only video files take the generation-backed route.
`npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed with zero errors, and `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx` passed with 4/4 tests green.
